series_metadata.series_description: Pursuant to state law and board rule, a license cannot be issued until the applicant submits to a criminal background check and the board deems that the results are consistent with licensure. Results are received in paper form in the board office reflecting background checks by the FBI and BCII.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Background check results are noted as having been received/reviewed via a checkmark in the application file. Retain FBI and BCII results 12 months after receipt in the office, then destroy, unless the results require to referral to enforcement, in which case the papers are retained according to enforcement file retention schedules., Paper: Shred
